title: ‘Tenet’ and the Oscars Are Delayed. What’s Next?
format: Tenet moves two weeks, the Oscars move two months, and Sean and Amanda try to figure out what a 2020 movie even is anymore.

segments.heading: Tenet's Two-Week Delay and the Domino Effect on Summer Movies

films.title: Tenet
films.note: Pushed two weeks, which Sean thinks is nowhere near enough runway for theaters to actually reopen safely.

films.title: Inception
films.note: Warner Bros. is re-releasing it July 17, the date Tenet vacated, as a 10-year-anniversary placeholder.

films.title: Mulan
films.note: Now technically the first wide theatrical release, July 24 — neither host expects that date to hold.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: Moved from August to October.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: The first major 2020 tentpole to move, now nudged five more days.

films.title: Fast & Furious 9
films.note: Universal moved it to April 2021, one of the earliest dominoes to fall.

films.title: Unhinged
films.note: Russell Crowe road-rage thriller, one of two "sacrificial lamb" films opening July 10 to test whether audiences will show up.

films.title: The Broken Hearts Gallery
films.note: The other July 10 test case; neither host could even find a trailer for it.

segments.heading: This Weekend's At-Home Triple Feature
segments.note: Netflix, VOD, and Disney+, three different paths around theaters

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Spike Lee's Netflix film; Amanda suspects it would've gotten a theatrical run and a real awards campaign in a normal year.

films.title: The King of Staten Island
films.note: Judd Apatow's Pete Davidson vehicle, $20 on VOD; Sean floats a real (if long-shot) awards case for Bill Burr and Marisa Tomei.

films.title: Artemis Fowl
films.note: Kenneth Branagh's Disney+ adaptation; Sean says it's worse than the reviews suggested and blames Josh Gad and a neutered, de-fanged lead.

segments.heading: Should Sean Go to Telluride? And What Cannes Did Instead
segments.note: festivals try to figure out what they are without a room to gather in

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: One of the films that made Cannes's 2020 label list even though the festival itself didn't happen.

segments.heading: Oscars Overhaul: A Diversity Task Force, 10 Best Picture Slots, and a New April Date
segments.note: eligibility window now runs through February 28, 2021

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: Widely seen as the film that would've taken a 10th Best Picture slot last year, had one existed.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Sean's example of the Academy finally getting international cinema right, after decades of not.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Comparison point for whether a superhero movie could ever compete again.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: Amanda's still looking forward to it, whenever it arrives; floated as a hypothetical beneficiary of the expanded field.

films.title: Never Rarely Sometimes Always
films.note: One of several early-2020 films Sean argues are worthy despite getting buried by the calendar reshuffle.

films.title: Natalie Wood: What Remains Behind
films.note: Amanda jokes about it being a redundantly titled documentary.

films.title: Crip Camp
films.note: Sundance doc already tabbed for Oscar attention, now with a much longer runway to stay in voters' minds.

films.title: On the Record
films.note: HBO Max documentary Sean wonders will be forgotten by the time the pushed-back season actually votes.

films.title: Miss Americana
films.note: The Taylor Swift documentary; premiered at Sundance, hit Netflix a couple weeks later.

films.title: Bad Boys for Life
films.note: Amanda's joke about "Bad Boys 3" losing Oscar eligibility became the running bit about how absurd the extended calendar is.

films.title: Zack Snyder's Justice League
films.note: Sean asks, half-seriously, if the Snyder Cut could be a Best Picture nominee. Amanda: no.

The French Dispatch.title: The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun
